page.title: Project problems: inherited apps, internal tools, integrations, and rescue work | Bc. Ondřej Halata (halatao.cz)
page.h1: Project situations that usually matter before the technology choice
page.description: Overview of problem pages covering inherited app takeover, internal tools, integrations, stalled projects, weak performance, and the need for senior contract capacity.
page.primaryQuery: Project situations that usually matter before the technology choice

hero.title: Start from the problem shape, not the implementation label
hero.subtitle: Useful when the delivery model is still unclear but the pressure inside the project is already real.

intro: These pages help buyers describe the situation and risk first, and only then choose the right service or technical direction.
The section covers inherited applications, internal tools, integrations, missing senior capacity, client portals, slow products, and incomplete projects that need to be rescued.

sections.title: What you will find here
body: Each child page explains the main symptoms, the cost of leaving the situation alone, what a strong fit looks like, and which next step usually makes sense.

sections.title: When to start here
body: This section works as a commercial starting point. It helps you choose the right collaboration model based on the actual project situation rather than a loose technology label.
bullets: teams that need to frame the problem before choosing a supplier model
projects where the delivery path is still unclear
buyers dealing with both technical and operational pressure
companies trying to improve the decision frame before budget or scope

sections.title: How to continue
body: The best next move is to open the page closest to your current situation and then continue into the linked problem, comparison, or inquiry path.
bullets: open the problem page closest to current reality
follow into the linked service or guide pages
move to inquiry when the situation is concrete enough to discuss directly

faq.question: Should I start here or on the services section?
faq.answer: Start here when the problem is clearer than the preferred delivery model.

faq.question: Are these pages only an SEO index?
faq.answer: No. They are written as real decision support for delivery buyers and connect directly to the commercial next step.

faq.question: Do the problem pages lead into services and inquiry?
faq.answer: Yes. Each one is wired into the relevant service, guide, and inquiry flow.

for: teams that need to frame the problem before choosing a supplier model
projects where the delivery path is still unclear
buyers dealing with both technical and operational pressure
companies trying to improve the decision frame before budget or scope
notFor: passive browsing with no concrete project question

cta.label: Explore the project fit
cta.note: A short summary of the current situation and target outcome is enough to suggest the next step.
